methodology.name: S.M.I.L.E.
methodology.fullName: Sustainable Methodology for Impact Lifecycle Enablement
methodology.principle: Impact first, data last. Start with the outcome, not the data. Outcome → Action → Insight → Information → Data.
methodology.author: Nicolas Waern, WINNIIO / LifeAtlas
methodology.philosophy: Absorb the past, simulate the future, to transcend the now. Companies and cities can simulate the future — but only if they start with impact, not data.

phases.name: Reality Emulation
phases.description: Create a shared reality canvas — establishing where, when, and who. The foundation is a real-world planetary foundation in 3D+, understandable by all. Everything in reality has had, has, or will occupy an area in space and time.
activities: Identify where and when on the planet
Stakeholder mapping (who needs to be involved)
phases.keyQuestion: In order to think outside the box, one has to define the box first. What is the starting point and boundary of your sociotechnological ecosystem?

phases.name: Concurrent Engineering
phases.description: Define the scope (as-is to to-be), invite stakeholders to innovate together, validate hypotheses virtually before committing resources. Virtual Minimal Viable Twinning (MVT) — test ideas in simulation before reality.
phases.keyQuestion: What does the Minimal Viable Twin look like, and how do we validate it virtually before investing in physical implementation?

phases.name: Collective Intelligence
phases.description: Connect physical sensors, meet initial KPIs, create ontologies for shared understanding. The ontology factory becomes the foundation for AI factories. Knowledge Graph ontology foundation — creating relationships between fragmented realities.
activities: Confirm operational metrics and business value
Align with existing data and industry standards (ISO)
phases.keyQuestion: How do we create ontology factories as the foundation for AI factories, connecting fragmented realities into a knowledge graph?

phases.name: Contextual Intelligence
phases.description: Connected everything — command & control, real-time decisions, uptime optimization, predictive analytics, root cause analysis. The digital twin becomes operationally aware of its context.
phases.keyQuestion: How do we enable real-time contextual awareness across the entire ecosystem — understanding not just what is happening, but why?

phases.name: Continuous Intelligence
phases.description: Leverage accumulated knowledge — prescriptive maintenance, AI-driven prognostics, universal event pipelines. Simulate everything. Identify black swans before they strike.
phases.keyQuestion: How do we leverage explainable AI factories for exponential scale, scope, and learning — moving from reactive to prescriptive?

phases.name: Perpetual Wisdom
phases.description: Share impact across the planet. Up-cycle exploration, ecosystem enablement, circular strategies, open-source contribution. Knowledge becomes perpetual — transferable across generations, industries, and geographies.
phases.keyQuestion: How do we ensure that knowledge created today is transferable, reusable, and contributes to planetary-scale impact?

perspectives.name: From People
perspectives.description: Organization agnostic. Focus on concurrent engineering, collective intelligence, stakeholder mapping, and digital twin enablement. People are actors in sociotechnological ecosystems continuously transferring knowledge.

perspectives.name: From Systems
perspectives.description: Planetary scope. Focus on operating context, standards identification, ontology alignment, and metadata mastery. Systems must be interoperable across technical, semantic, and organizational layers.

perspectives.name: From Planet
perspectives.description: GIS, CIM, BIM, satellite data, reality canvas. The planetary foundation — understanding spatial and temporal context at global scale. Everything in reality occupies space and time.

aiJourney.name: Data Contextualization
aiJourney.description: Raw data is contextualized — understanding what it means in its spatial, temporal, and organizational context.

aiJourney.name: AI-Ready
aiJourney.description: Data is structured, tagged, and accessible for AI consumption. Ontologies and metadata are in place.
aiJourney.decisionMaking: Human Decision Making with AI support

aiJourney.name: AI-Infused
aiJourney.description: AI augments human decision-making with predictions, recommendations, and pattern recognition.

aiJourney.name: AI-Ingrained
aiJourney.description: AI is embedded in operations — autonomous decisions within defined boundaries, continuous learning.

aiJourney.name: Explainable AI Decision Making
aiJourney.description: Full autonomous decision-making that is explainable, auditable, and anchored in reality. Explainability is easiest when anchored in real data and real outcomes.

keyConcepts.name: Actor-Network Theory (ANT)
keyConcepts.description: Introduced by Bruno Latour. Both humans and non-humans (technologies, objects) are actors in networks that shape outcomes. Social, technical, and natural worlds are interconnected. In SMILE, this means every participant — human, technological, or organizational — contributes to the ecosystem.

keyConcepts.name: Minimal Interoperability Mechanisms (MIMs)
keyConcepts.description: Context management framework for sociotechnological ecosystems. Designing systems that acknowledge the active role of different actors (sensors, people, institutions) and their interrelationships. Facilitates seamless data integration and collaboration across domains.

keyConcepts.name: Ontology Factories
keyConcepts.description: The foundation for AI factories. Creating structured knowledge representations (ontologies) that map relationships between fragmented realities. Knowledge Graph ontology foundations enable AI systems to reason about the real world.

keyConcepts.name: Minimal Viable Twin (MVT)
keyConcepts.description: The digital twin equivalent of MVP. Start with the smallest viable twin that delivers value, then iterate. Virtual first — validate in simulation before physical deployment.

keyConcepts.name: Virtual First Strategy
keyConcepts.description: Test everything in simulation before reality. Assess initiatives in a simulated environment to reduce risk. Try before you buy. 100% virtual first completion before physical deployment.

keyConcepts.name: Reality Canvas
keyConcepts.description: A real-world planetary foundation in 3D+, understandable by all. The spatial-temporal anchor for all digital twin work. Establishes where, when, and the boundary of the ecosystem.

keyConcepts.name: Omni-Interoperability
keyConcepts.description: Accelerating knowledge transfer between people, systems, and AI across four layers: legal/organizational, semantic, syntactic, and technical. Where everything is understood between actors, irrespective of whether they are human, machines, or autonomous AI agents.

keyConcepts.name: Edge-Native Intelligence
keyConcepts.description: Edge capabilities same as the cloud. Intelligence should be created where data is generated — distributed intelligence via edge/hybrid, 5G/6G, network slicing. Local control first — digital twins on the edge.

keyConcepts.name: Phoenix Strategies
keyConcepts.description: Perpetual rebirth and renewal. Knowledge and systems designed to be decomposable, reusable, and recyclable. When one system reaches end of life, its knowledge feeds the next generation.

interoperabilityLayers.name: Legal & Organizational Interoperability
interoperabilityLayers.description: Organization & process alignment. Legal frameworks, governance, data sovereignty, GDPR/personal data dimensions.

interoperabilityLayers.name: Semantic Interoperability
interoperabilityLayers.description: Shared meaning across systems and actors through metadata registries and ontologies. Ensuring all parties interpret data the same way.

interoperabilityLayers.name: Syntactic Interoperability
interoperabilityLayers.description: Data format and structure alignment. Ensuring systems can parse and exchange data in agreed-upon formats regardless of implementation.

interoperabilityLayers.name: Technical Interoperability
interoperabilityLayers.description: Transport and connectivity. The technical protocols and infrastructure enabling data exchange between systems.
